articles.title: Oil: Strait of Hormuz risks keep prices supported – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Geoff Yu highlights that Oil is back in focus as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz nears a standstill and ceasefire risks rise. iFlow data show energy equities flows stabilizing after June profit-taking, with valuations and under-ownership becoming more attractive.

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Pound consolidates after hitting fresh all-time highs at 218.00
articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) is pulling lower against the Japanese Yen (JPY) on Thursday, after hitting a fresh all-time high at 218.01 earlier on the day.

articles.title: US Dollar: Fed minutes flag supply-driven inflation risks – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists highlight that the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) Minutes showed rising concern over inflation risks, even as the United States (US) labor market remains stable.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar extends rebound after RBNZ rate hike, US-Iran tensions cap gains
articles.expand: NZD/USD advances on Thursday and trades around 0.5730 at the time of writing, up 0.56% on the day.

articles.title: The US Dollar Consolidates with a Softer Bias as Oil Steadies
articles.expand: With the fog of war still shrouding the near-term outlook, the US dollar is mostly consolidating with a softer bias against the G10 currencies. Outside of the New Zealand dollar that is still basking in yesterday's rate hike, and the Canadian dollar, which is slightly softer, the other G10 currencies are up less than 0.15% ahead of the North American open. August WTI and September Brent are trading within yesterday’s ranges. The FOMC minutes released yesterday failed to tell the market anything it did not already know. Fed officials are divided, which had already been revealed in the “dot plot...

articles.title: GBP/CAD Hits Decade High as USMCA Shock Adds New Driver Ahead of Jobs Data
articles.expand: GBP/CAD climbed to its highest level in a decade this week, reflecting an increasingly powerful divergence between a Pound supported by fading domestic political risks and a Canadian Dollar facing mounting structural headwinds. articles.title: Euro advances against Canadian Dollar on strong German trade, weak oil
articles.expand: EUR/CAD gains ground after two days of losses, trading around 1.6210 during the European hours on Thursday. The currency cross remains stronger following stronger-than-expected trade data from Germany.

articles.title: Euro: ECB hawkish repricing with bond selloff – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank Research’s Early Morning Reid notes that European assets are sensitive to the renewed energy shock. Market pricing for ECB hikes by December rose sharply, implying a chance of three hikes this year after June’s move.

articles.title: Australian Dollar steadies above 0.6900 as markets price a negotiated end to Iran’s war
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar (AUD) keeps trading sideways against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday, showing a surprising resilience to the escalating tensions in Iran.

articles.title: Gold clings to gains on weaker USD; bearish bias remains amid Iran risks, Fed hike bets
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its bid tone above the $4,100 mark through the first half of the European session on Thursday and looks to build on the previous day's bounce from a one-week low.

articles.title: Japanese Yen remains pressured close to 40-year lows as intervention risks linger
articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) remains locked in a high-stakes standoff against the US Dollar, testing the ultimate limits of Tokyo’s tolerance for its local currency to depreciate.

articles.title: Japanese Yen gains against US Dollar despite renewed geopolitical risks
articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) trades higher against the US Dollar (USD) despite renewed geopolitical risks.

articles.title: Euro: Early gains against US Dollar at risk on Fed story – ING
articles.expand: Chris Turner at ING highlights that EUR/USD has held up despite higher Oil, as Euro swap rates outperformed US rates on expectations of an ECB hike in September. However, he argues the Fed narrative will dominate, with EUR/USD likely to surrender gains and fall below 1.14.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Euro wavers around 1.1430 with the bearish trend intact
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) posts moderate gains against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday, hitting session highs near 1.1440, yet trapped within the weekly range, with the broader bearish trend in play.

articles.title: British Pound extends rally vs JPY; climbs to fresh high since January 2008

articles.title: Japan may be changing its Yen strategy, but markets don’t look scared
articles.expand: Japan may be changing its intervention playbook, but that might not be enough to rescue the battered Yen. With USD/JPY hovering at four-decade highs, the currency’s weakness is being driven less by speculative pressure and more by a powerful structural force: the wide US-Japan rate gap.

articles.title: FOMC June Meeting Minutes Confirm Divided Fed
articles.expand: The minutes of the Fed’s June meeting revealed division over whether inflation will cool as the Middle East conflict eases or stay elevated as AI-related investment keeps pressure on prices.

articles.title: Financial & Forex Market Recap – July 8, 2026
articles.expand: Trump called the U.S.-Iran ceasefire “over” after fresh strikes, sending oil higher and risk assets lower. Fed minutes lifted hike bets and yields, but the dollar still slipped.

articles.title: Fed Minutes Confirm Mixed Views About Outlook For Rates
articles.expand: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released the minutes of its June 16-17 monetary policy meeting, which marked Kevin Warsh's first meeting as head of the central bank. Amid considerable uncertainty surrounding the outlook for inflation, the minutes said participants discussed a range of scenarios for the evolution of the economy and for future monetary policy actions.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Bears now look at the 200-day SMA
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar remains unable to find sustainable upside traction, keeping its trade below the key 0.7000 yardstick for now. So far, the constructive tone should remain unchallenged while above its critical 200-day SMA.

articles.title: 2.50%: Why the Kiwi's first hike in three years is a wager on a number nobody can see
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) raised the Official Cash Rate (OCR) by 25 basis points to 2.50% at 02:00 GMT on Wednesday, its first hike in three years and the moment the bank that cut deeper than any G10 peer last cycle turned to face the other way.

articles.title: Dollar Climbs on Escalation of Middle East Hostilities

articles.title: RBNZ Raises OCR to 2.50%
articles.expand: The RBNZ raised its Official Cash Rate to 2.50%, its first hike in three years, but won’t commit to a timeline for what comes next.

articles.title: Australian Dollar After RBA's Hunter Hawkish Remarks
articles.expand: The Australian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday, as the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) kept the door open for additional monetary policy tightening, if necessary, to bring inflation back to the central bank's target.

articles.title: New Zealand Central Bank Hikes Rate For First Time Since 2023
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand raised its benchmark interest rate for the first time in more than three years to bring inflation to target and hinted at further tightening. The Monetary Policy Committee, governed by Anna Breman, decided to lift the Official Cash Rate by 25 basis points to 2.50 percent. This was the first hike since May 2023.

articles.expand: The New Zealand dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday.

articles.expand: The New Zealand dollar received the boost that normally accompanies a rate hike, but the rally quickly lost momentum. articles.title: RBA’s Hunter: Supply Shocks Don’t Mean Inflation Can Be Ignored

articles.expand: Bank of Japan board member Toichiro Asada said he would only support another interest rate hike once inflation is clearly being driven by stronger domestic demand rather than temporary cost pressures. articles.title: ESRB warns of vulnerabilities in the financial system linked to frontier AI models
articles.expand: The European Systemic Risk Board (ESRB), the EU’s macroprudential oversight authority, is issuing a formal warning regarding vulnerabilities in the financial system linked to frontier AI models. The ESRB's warning calls for relevant authorities to reflect these risks in supervisory and oversight work.

articles.expand: European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta said the Eurozone economy continues to face a delicate balance between persistent inflation risks and weakening growth, arguing that policymakers should avoid committing to any predetermined path for interest rates. “The outlook remains fragile. articles.title: The US Dollar just beat the Swiss Franc at its own safe-haven game
articles.expand: As the king among safe havens, the Swiss Franc (CHF) is supposed to benefit from geopolitical shocks such as the Iran war. This time, it didn’t.
